intent: A tclass=capability2 denial (systemd_resolved_t denied bpf). Exercises the newer capability2 class and its cap2-common perm vocabulary (bpf), distinct from the original capability class.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic). For decline scenarios these MAY disagree on purpose.

oracle.narrower_than_R_by: As with the capability class, audit2allow -R emits NO macro for a capability2 self-grant - it produces the identical raw self-allow as -N. Honest datapoint: the macro baseline is not broader here.
oracle.audit2why_note: audit2why reports 'Missing type enforcement (TE) allow rule' - a clean TeAllowable.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A parser that only knows the original capability class (cap common) will not recognize capability2/bpf. capability2 is a distinct class with its own cap2 common (bpf, perfmon, syslog, ...). The narrow fix must target capability2 and use the cap2 perm.
